mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit availability signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and RSAFS declines shows no predictive power for SPX direction over 20-day windows, with directional accuracy failing to exceed 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising housing starts indicate resilient consumer demand that offsets tightening financial conditions, supporting equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive SPX forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with correlation coefficient dropping below 0.1 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter reflects expanding corporate revenue bases, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, with t-statistic magnitude remaining below 1.5 across all rolling 10-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 250,000 per month signals robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending, lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 250k fails to predict positive SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or forward return median remains negative across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling consumer credit creates a credit-fueled inventory buildup that eventually triggers margin compression and equity drawdowns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO growth and RSAFS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns, with directional accuracy failing to exceed 50% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, simultaneous Fed rate cuts act as a liquidity backstop that stabilizes equity markets and generates positive forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0% alongside falling FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year window.
